ocr.text: In the evolving landscape of the North American automotive industry, the interplay between vehicle sales and research and
development (R&D) expenditures is becoming increasingly critical. The table presented herein illustrates the vehicle sales
volume by model alongside corresponding autonomous driving R&D spend, providing insights into market performance and
investment strategies. For instance, ModelA achieved a sales volume of 150,000 units in 2023, reflecting a 25% increase from
the previous year, while the total revenue generated from this model reached $30,000. However, not all models have performed
favorably; for ModelH, the sales volume decreased to 11,000 units, translating to a change of -5%. This decline raises questions
about the effectiveness of current R&D strategies, particularly as expenditures on autonomous driving technology remain a
focal point. Notably, the R&D spend for ModelD, which has been rebranded as ModelE, was recorded at $300K, indicating a
need for strategic reassessments in the competitive landscape.

Looking at broader trends, the total FY2023 revenue is projected to reach 1.2 billion USD, with safety protocols accounting for
15% of this total, highlighting the significance of integrating advanced safety features into new models. In this context, the
industry must balance innovation with consumer demand to thrive in a rapidly changing marketplace.
